Religious order.24072: Augustinian order, "deodonades" (this is a mendicant order, not to be confused with Augustinian canonesses)
Religious order.24071: Augustinian Deodonades (this is a mendicant order, not to be confused with Augustinian canonesses)
Religious order.24104: Benedictine after second founding (original order uncertain)
Religious order.24309: Poor Clares, then Cistercian after 1515 (May have also had Benedictine rule at some point)
Religious order.24151: Benedictine, then Poor Clares after c. 1350
Religious order.24133: Benedictine until 1437; Jesuits (male) lived there from 1454-98; Poor Clares from 1498; Dominicans from 1560-75
Religious order.24145: Benedictine, then Dominican Tertiaries some time before 1441
Religious order.24289: Originally Franciscan Tertiaries, then Poor Clares
Religious order.24149: Benedictine, then later Dominican as of 1257
Religious order.24067: Augustinian / MOP (minor order of penitents?) after 1441
Religious order.24288: Originally Augustinian [?]; Franciscan Third Order since 1444
Religious order.24335: The first order is unknown. In 1328 the community is Benedictine. In 1256 it is referred to as Benedictine as well.
Religious order.24257: Hospital Order of S. Mary of Bethlehem
Religious order.24308: Poor Clares, then Benedictine after 1513 (needs verification)
Religious order.24346: Women’s Benedictine & Men’s Monastery (double, 7th–9th c.; became men’s)
Religious order.24106: Benedictine as of 1275; possibly originally Basilian
Religious order.24264: Humiliati until 1591, when the hospital was supressed, and became a Benedictine house
Religious order.24105: Benedictine as of 1000 (possible earlier orders uncertain)
Religious order.24250: Gilbertine (Dugdale says that the order is Sempringham)
Religious order.24338: Thought to be Augustinian; Further research is required to verify this.
Religious order.24334: The first order is assumed to be Augustinian because the hopital was a dependency of Southwork priory which was Augustinian and followed the Augustinian Rule.
Religious order.24304: Poor Clares -- became Lateran Canonesses when they merged with S. Trinita (see "Incorporated By," below).
Religious order.24125: Benedictine nuns (St. Edmund, medieval, now lost)
Religious order.24159: Benedictine/ Cistercian in the later half of the 15th century. It claimed to be Cistercian for some part of the 13th century.
Religious order.24155: Benedictine, then Santiago (miltary order). NB: this is the only feminine community that the latter had in all of Catalonia
Religious order.24255: Hospital (not a true monastery; later almshouse)
Religious order.24108: Benedictine monks (St. Mary’s Priory, 1123); also hospital for women (late medieval)
Religious order.24128: Benedictine nuns (St. Mary’s Priory, founded before 1123)
Religious order.24109: Benedictine monks, with women’s cell (Vowesses, 14th–16th c.)
Religious order.24211: College of secular canons (women’s community in early medieval, became secular)
